question: How to align shoulders and hips?
content:
Aligning your shoulders and hips correctly is crucial for a consistent and powerful delivery. Generally, you want your shoulders and hips to be square to your target line, meaning they are parallel to the foul line and facing directly down the lane towards your intended mark. This alignment promotes a straight swing path and prevents unnecessary body rotation that can pull your shot off target. As you initiate your approach, maintain this square alignment for as long as possible. During the downswing, your hips will naturally open slightly to allow for a free arm swing, but your shoulders should remain relatively square until the release. Practice feeling this alignment throughout your entire delivery.

For bowlers who don't bowl straight and hook the ball, we suggest to use the E.T.T. (Everything Towards the Target) system. That will help your swing remain straight and always pointing your breakpoint.
